subject: Project Management
title: Planning Poker
description: A fast breakdown of Planning Poker for builders.

hook.text: In Planning Poker, each team member holds up a card with their estimate — simultaneously.

buildup.text: Cards show relative sizes: 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 13, 21. The numbers follow the Fibonacci sequence.

discovery.text: Revealing cards simultaneously prevents anchoring — nobody's estimate is influenced by hearing others first.

twist.text: When estimates wildly disagree, the team discusses why. This conversation is often more valuable than the number.

climax.text: Planning Poker turns estimation from a solo guess into a structured team discussion.

punchline.text: The best estimate comes from the disagreement, not the average.

quiz.question: Why do Planning Poker cards get revealed simultaneously?
options: To prevent anchoring — so no one is influenced by hearing others first
Because it's more fun that way
